text: The kidney is positioned more medially, while the tumor is located more laterally, highlighting their spatial relationship during the procedure.
The surgeon uses intraoperative ultrasound to identify the margins of the tumor, followed by the application of a cord to further demarcate these margins for clearer visualization.
The surgeon inserts the RFA ablator along the demarcated area, approximately one centimeter away from the tumor margins, and performs ablation on the targeted tissue.
The surgeon uses the RFA ablator to ablate a large circumferential area, approximately one centimeter in width, around the demarcated margins of the tumor.
The surgeon uses a bi-clamp device as a bipolar coagulator to coagulate the blood vessels, reducing bleeding in the surgical field.
The surgeon uses sharp dissection with scissors to carefully free necrosed or fibrosed bands, separating them from surrounding tissue.
The surgeon uses hydrodissection to wash away the kidney parenchyma, alternating with the use of a bipolar clamp and scissors for dissection.
The surgeon performs a slow dissection through the renal parenchyma, maintaining clear margins away from the tumor.
The surgeon alternates between using the bipolar clamp, scissors, and RFA ablator to carefully remove the tumor, ensuring large margins are maintained around the excised tissue.
The surgeon uses sharp dissection and cautery to free the tumor posteriorly. The excised specimen is then placed into a retrieval bag.
The surgeon takes several margins from the residual tissue to verify complete removal of the tumor, ensuring no remaining cancerous tissue.
The surgeon uses an argon laser to cauterize the entire surface of the defect, ensuring hemostasis and sealing the tissue edges.
The surgeon places a buttress suture to secure a surgical bolster over the area of the defect.
The surgeon places the surgery cell bolster within the buttress suture and applies flow seal at the base of the bolster before cinching it down.
The surgeon cinches down the surgery cell bolster and applies a layer of tissue liberally over the defect and bolster to achieve a secure seal.
